title: Fee calculation divides before multiplying, zeroing the fee on small withdrawals
description: `withdraw()` computes `fee = amount / 10_000 * feeBps`. For any `amount` less than `10_000 / feeBps` (~333 wei at the default 30 bps), the first division rounds to zero and the subsequent multiplication leaves the fee at zero. A griefer can withdraw in a loop of dust-sized amounts and avoid paying any protocol fee.
impact: Protocol loses fee revenue on small withdrawals. Scaled up with many transactions or used intentionally, this results in the fee being routinely bypassed and the `feeSink` receiving nothing.
recommendation: Reorder operations so multiplication happens before division: `uint256 fee = (amount * feeBps) / 10_000;`. Since Solidity 0.8 is overflow-checked, the intermediate product is safe for any `amount <= type(uint256).max / feeBps`.
